title: ajuga vulgaris subsp. reptans
intro: Ajuga vulgaris subsp. reptans, also known as bugleweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of bugleweed include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats. Bugleweed can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
